channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Defying logic - how Haaland could end up with 260 international goals
item.description: Erling Haaland delivers on the biggest stage yet for Norway - but just how many goals could he end up with in international football?

item.title: Koeman resigns, while Netherlands report racist abuse
item.description: Ronald Koeman resigns as Netherlands manager after their World Cup exit, which led to "appalling" racist abuse aimed at players who missed penalties.

item.title: Quansah and James miss England training as right-back problems mount
item.description: Jarell Quansah and Reece James miss training as England's right-back issues mount before their World Cup last-32 match against DR Congo on Wednesday.

item.title: Mbappe scores twice as France beat Sweden
item.description: Kylian Mbappe scores twice as France cruise to a comfortable win over Sweden in their World Cup round of 32 match at the New York New Jersey Stadium.

item.title: Spurs agree £85m deal for West Ham's Fernandes
item.description: Tottenham agree a deal to sign West Ham's Mateus Fernandes as Manchester United pull out of the race for midfielder.

item.title: Bigger than Rumble in the Jungle? DR Congo bid for World Cup history
item.description: DR Congo's run at the 2026 World Cup comes 52 years on from both their disastrous debut at the finals and one of sport's greatest occasions held in Kinshasa.

item.title: Haaland the late hero as Norway knock out resilient Ivory Coast
item.description: Manchester City striker Erling Haaland scores a late winner as Norway beat Ivory Coast 2-1 to book their place in the last 16 in the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: England fans' behaviour 'excellent' at World Cup
item.description: England supporters in the United States are praised for their "excellent" behaviour during the group stage of the World Cup, but domestic incidents are up on recent tournaments.

item.title: Coventry boss Lampard signs new contract to 2029
item.description: Coventry City manager Frank Lampard signs a new contract until 2029 before the club's first Premier League campaign in 25 years.

item.title: PSG set Barcola price - Wednesday's gossip
item.description: Paris St-Germain set their price for Bradley Barcola, Chelsea want Bournemouth striker Eli Junior Kroupi, Ayyoub Bouaddi is attracting interest, plus more.

item.title: 'Goal of the tournament' - Amad's brilliant equaliser for Ivory Coast
item.description: Amad Diallo scores a "goal of the tournament" contender to equalise for Ivory Coast against Norway in their last-32 match.

item.title: Barcola 'emphatically' doubles France's lead over Sweden
item.description: Bradley Barcola "empahatically" fires home France's second goal in their World Cup round of 32 match against Sweden at the New York New Jersey Stadium.

item.title: 'A moment of real quality' - Nusa's sensational strike puts Norway ahead
item.description: Norway take the lead in their round of 32 tie against Ivory Coast after a brilliant strike from forward Antonio Nusa.

item.title: Germany beaten on penalties as Paraguay reach last 16
item.description: Four-time winners Germany are knocked out of the 2026 World Cup, losing to Paraguay 4-3 on penalties, following a 1-1 draw after extra time.

item.title: Martinelli scores stoppage-time winner to send Brazil through to last 16
item.description: Gabriel Martinelli scores a dramatic 95th-minute winner with Brazil coming from behind to beat Japan, set up a World Cup last-16 tie against Norway or Ivory Coast.

item.title: Villa among four Premier League clubs fined by Uefa over squad-cost rules
item.description: Aston Villa have been fined 22.5m euros (£19.4m) by Uefa for a "significant breach" of its squad-cost rule for 2025.

item.title: France superstars thriving thanks to Deschamps' bold changes
item.description: Why Didier Deschamps' changes to personnel and formation could propel 2022 runners-up France to go one better in this World Cup.

item.title: Brighton sign Leeds defender Struijk for about £20m
item.description: Brighton sign Leeds United defender Pascal Struijk for an undisclosed fee understood to be about £20m.

item.title: Arsenal consider Barcola as alternative to Rogers
item.description: Arsenal are expected to step up their interest in Paris Saint-Germain's Bradley Barcola this week after receiving encouragement that the forward could be available this summer.

item.title: Germany fans in need of hope as prospect of Klopp looms
item.description: As Germany again fail to progress at a World Cup BBC Sport looks at the fallout from their defeat by Paraguay and what happens next.

item.title: Rosenior nears management return at Paris FC
item.description: Former Chelsea manager Liam Rosenior is set to return to football at French club Paris FC on a two-year contract, with the option of a further season.

item.title: Coach played mind games with Cusack before death, inquest told
item.description: A team-mate tells an inquest Maddy Cusack was "anxious" about Jonathan Morgan joining Sheffield United.

item.title: Where to watch the World Cup on the BBC and ITV
item.description: All the TV and radio details for each of the 2026 World Cup knockout games.

item.title: Catch up on the World Cup without any spoilers
item.description: It is a dilemma plenty of fans in the UK will have - and we are giving you the chance to follow the World Cup without spoilers.

item.title: The Paraguay hero who sold his kit when his son was born
item.description: Orlando Gill has become Paraguay's World Cup hero but was in football obscurity 18 months ago and had to sell his kit to support his family after the premature birth of his son.

item.title: Pick Mainoo over Anderson against DR Congo - Rooney
item.description: Former England captain Wayne Rooney says he would start Manchester United midfielder Kobbie Mainoo instead of Elliot Anderson in Wednesday's World Cup last-32 tie with DR Congo.

item.title: Tottenham goalkeeper Kinsky signs new deal
item.description: Tottenham Hotspur goalkeeper Antonin Kinsky signs a new, improved contract with the Premier League club.

item.title: Ecuador make Mexico fans hotel noise complaint
item.description: Ecuador make a complaint to World Cup organisers over noisy local fans outside their team hotel before their last-32 match against co-hosts Mexico.

item.title: AC Milan sign PSG's Portugal striker Ramos for £60m
item.description: AC Milan sign Paris St-Germain striker Goncalo Ramos for a reported club record transfer fee of about £60m.

item.title: Why new Scotland head coach has tricky task
item.description: Steve Clarke's successor as Scotland head coach may have a tricky task to sustain the national side's success over the long term.

item.title: Arsenal poised - can Newcastle convince Guimaraes for a second time?
item.description: Bruno Guimaraes has been Newcastle United's leader, but Arsenal are lurking with intent and could soon try to prise him away. What happens next, and could he yet stay put?

item.title: 'Terrible decision' - VAR controversially disallows Germany goal
item.description: Germany's Jonathan Tah has an extra-time goal controversially disallowed by VAR for a foul by Waldemar Anton on Paraguay goalkeeper Orlando Gill.

item.title: 'Germany play one way - and it doesn't work any more'
item.description: The team in the studio talk about what went wrong for Germany as they go out of the World Cup to Paraguay

item.title: Canale scores winning penalty after Tah blazes over as Germany knocked out
item.description: Four-time winners Germany are knocked out of the 2026 World Cup on penalties as Jonathan Tah blazes over in sudden death before Jose Canale scores the winning penalty.

item.title: Relive thrilling penalty shootout between Germany and Paraguay
item.description: Watch every penalty from Germany's last 32 match against Paraguay in the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: 'They needed that' - Havertz levels for Germany with header
item.description: Kai Havertz equalises for Germany against Paraquay in their last-32 tie.

item.title: 'That might just wake Germany up' - Paraguay take shock lead through Enciso
item.description: Julio Enciso gives Paraguay the lead as they look to upset the odds by defeating Germany in the round of 32.

item.title: Fuchs blows kiss to bowler after smashing six into river on cricket debut
item.description: Just a day after resigning as Newport County head coach, Christian Fuchs blows a kiss to a bowler after hitting him for six while making his cricket debut for Derbyshire village side Grindleford.

item.title: BBC Sport expert Sutton predicts World Cup's last-32 matches
item.description: BBC Sport's football expert Chris Sutton gives his predictions for the last-32 ties at the 2026 World Cup, including England against DR Congo.

item.title: Could set-piece coach be Celtic's key signing?
item.description: BBC Scotland crunches the numbers behind Ross Grant's set-piece impact at Hearts and looks at what he could bring to Celtic.

item.title: Who am I? Guess World Cup star No 23
item.description: Will you get today's player in as few attempts as possible?

item.title: Emotional Gakpo scores days after loss of unborn son
item.description: Liverpool forward Cody Gakpo scores for the Netherlands days after the loss of his unborn son, as they are knocked out on penalties by Morocco.

item.title: US security chief 'danced happy dance' after Iran exit
item.description: The United States' head of homeland security says he "danced a happy dance" when Iran's elimination from the World Cup was confirmed.

item.title: 45 minutes from Brazil 'humiliation' - but 'Crafty Carlo' does it again
item.description: Half-time in Houston and Brazil's players trudged off facing another early World Cup exit. But we should have known better than to doubt Carlo Ancelotti.

item.title: Should teams bring on players just for penalty shootouts?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at the numbers behind late substitutes taking penalties in shootouts.

item.title: 'Germany no longer first-class team ' - is this the end for Nagelsmann?
item.description: Germany make a shock last-32 exit against Paraguay, losing a World Cup penalty shootout for the first time to pile pressure on boss Julian Nagelsmann.

item.title: What you need to know about England's opponents DR Congo
item.description: England will face DR Congo in the round of 32. Here is what you need to know about them.

item.title: Chelsea's George close to permanent Everton move
item.description: Everton close in on a deal to sign Chelsea winger Tyrique George following his loan spell.

item.title: 'A joke' - how a 'terrible' VAR disallowed goal cost Germany
item.description: Alan Shearer, Jurgen Klopp, Pat Nevin and Darren Cann feel Germany were hard done by as a VAR call rules out a potential winning goal before they lose on penalties to Paraguay.

item.title: 'Indescribably hurt' Son apologises for South Korea World Cup exit
item.description: Son Heung-min apologises for South Korea's early World Cup exit and says he will "run to death" for the nation's football fans.

item.title: Ranking 10 stars who could be at their last World Cup
item.description: A number of the world's best players are likely playing in their last World Cup. Here's how I think they rank.

item.title: How to win a World Cup penalty shootout
item.description: We have a look at what can be learned from every World Cup penalty taken in a shootout ever.

item.title: England last-32 tie on BBC - see full schedule
item.description: England's World Cup last-32 game against DR Congo will be shown live on BBC One and BBC iPlayer, with all 16 first knockout round ties covered across BBC Sport.

item.title: Pampered, protected & primed - so why didn't Scotland players perform?
item.description: Scotland's players got everything they asked for at this World Cup. So why did they not perform on the pitch in the US?

item.title: Best team, moments, matches and players of World Cup group stage
item.description: As the World Cup group stages come to an end, BBC Sport reporters pick their highlights so far.

item.title: 'Bye-bye, Scotland' - Clarke's leaving letter to Tartan Army
item.description: Steve Clarke's letter to the Scotland fans in full following his departure as head coach.

item.title: Why are World Cup underdogs doing so well?
item.description: Are the surprise results achieved by lower-ranked teams in the World Cup a matter of luck - or clever planning and execution?

item.title: All done deals in June 2026
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: Who has been eliminated from the World Cup?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team lists the full set of nations who have been eliminated from the 2026 World Cup so far

item.title: How are the TV picks decided for the World Cup's round of 32?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains the complexities behind television selections for the 2026 World Cup's round of 32 fixtures

item.title: Is five-match ban for Qatar's Madibo excessive?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team look at whether Madibo's punishment for his challenge on Kone was proportionate.

item.title: What is the World Cup prize money?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team break down the prize money on offer at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Who is the African World Cup goalscorer older than Ronaldo and Messi?
item.description: While Cristiano Ronaldo and Lionel Messi appear to be in a personal battle to rewrite the World Cup record books, there is one landmark that appears beyond them.

item.title: What is BBC's 3D Live Match Experience and how does it work?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains the unique way of following matches during the 2026 World Cup via the BBC

item.title: Why Saudi Arabia and Iraq flags are not laid down at World Cup
item.description: The flags of Saudi Arabia and Iraq are not laid on the ground during World Cup pre-match ceremonies because they include religious wording.

item.title: What are World Cup's red card suspension rules?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains Fifa's suspension rules on red and yellow cards during the World Cup.

item.title: Rangers buy Hull goalkeeper Pandur - what will he bring?
item.description: Rangers sign Croatian goalkeeper Ivor Pandur from Hull City for an undisclosed fee - what will he bring to Ibrox?

item.title: 'Pandur poised for Rangers as Butland heads for Hull' - gossip
item.description: Rangers are reportedly poised to sign Hull City goalkeeper Ivor Pandur, with Jack Butland moving in the opposite direction, as Aberdeen, Dundee, Dundee United and Motherwell are said to have received bids for key players.

item.title: Ex-Everton defender Godfrey joins Rangers on loan from Atalanta
item.description: Former Everton defender Ben Godfrey is determined to help Rangers "achieve what it deserves" after signing on loan from Atalanta.

item.title: Spurs sign Norway keeper Panengstuen from SK Brann
item.description: Norwegian international goalkeeper Selma Panengstuen says it is a "dream" move to join Women's Super League side Tottenham.

item.title: Kerr joins NWSL side Gotham FC
item.description: Australian international Sam Kerr has joined Gotham FC on a four-year deal following her departure from Chelsea.

item.title: Pattinson signs new deal with London City Lionesses
item.description: London City Lionesses has announced England defender Poppy Pattinson has extended her contract until summer 2029.

item.title: Football triplets make historic debut for Guernsey
item.description: A set of 16-year-old triplets make their debut for Guernsey as the island side finishes third in the Cherry Godfrey Cup.

item.title: Double Ballon d'Or winner Putellas to join London City Lionesses
item.description: Two-time Ballon d'Or winner Alexia Putellas decides to move to London City Lionesses after leaving Barcelona.

item.title: Graham agrees permanent deal with Ipswich Women
item.description: Ipswich Town Women sign forward Kit Graham on a two-year contract following a successful loan from Tottenham last season.

item.title: How Cornish miners brought football to Mexico
item.description: As Mexico prepares to become the first country to host or co-host the World Cup three times, BBC Sport looks at its Cornish footballing roots.

item.title: The summer a golden generation lost its shine
item.description: The summer of 2006 was meant to be England's moment. A world-class squad, celebrity partners and confident fans - the hype was huge and the fall was hard.

item.title: How Toone is navigating grief through football
item.description: England and Manchester United midfielder Ella Toone opens up about grief, football and preparing for her wedding without her dad in a new BBC documentary.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.
